key_insight: A coupled LSTM + physics-informed GNN can reconstruct nonlinear, history-dependent local stress fields by separating temporal constitutive memory from spatial equilibrium enforcement.
engineering_relevance: This enables fast surrogate stress-field prediction for heterogeneous microstructures, offering large speedups over finite element analysis while preserving accuracy across different mesh resolutions and element types.
method: LSTM encodes macroscopic stress-strain loading history into a compact latent state
Physics-informed GNN reconstructs the spatial stress field from mesh connectivity at each time step
Training uses a warm-up relative loss to balance reconstruction error and discrete equilibrium penalty
evidence_signal: Trained on 10,000 non-proportional loading paths for a plate-with-a-hole elasto-plastic microstructure, the model achieved about three orders of magnitude speedup and 1.9% cumulative error on sequences twice as long as training.
watch_item: Performance depends on careful loss-weight scheduling; fixed weighting can fail in the elasto-plastic regime, so convergence and transferability should be checked for new constitutive settings.
paper_title: Non-linear mechanical field reconstruction coupling recurrent neural networks with physics-informed graph neural networks
